RepresentativeInfo.CapitolAddress: MO House of Representatives, 201 West Capitol Avenue, Room 118-CC, Jefferson City MO 65101
RepresentativeInfo.Biography:
Representative Terri Violet, a Republican, proudly represents part of St. Charles County (District 104) in the Missouri House of Representatives. She was elected to her first two-year term in November 2024.
Rep. Violet served our country in the United States Navy, before being honorably discharged. She is also deeply proud of her rich family heritage, which includes a remarkable connection to American history. She is a descendant of George Soule, one of the original passengers aboard the Mayflower, linking her family to the early foundations of our nation.
Outside of her legislative responsibilities, Rep. Violet is an active member of First Baptist Church in St. Charles. She values spending quality time with her husband, Jeff, and their two children, Tiffany and Matt.

SponsoredBill.LongTitle: Modifies provisions relating to epinephrine products
Includes occupational therapists in the list of health care providers authorized to issue physician's statements for the issuance of disabled placards and license plates
Establishes provisions relating to a freestanding claim of actual innocence in a postconviction action or proceeding challenging the validity of a judgment
Creates provisions relating to taxes for emergency services
Provides for the distribution of certain moneys for a memorial or museum dedicated to veterans in Perry County

CoSponsoredBill.LongTitle: Changes the law regarding firearm concealed carry permits
Creates the offense of and civil penalties for disclosure of intimate digital depictions
Modifies provisions relating to public safety
Provides protections against discrimination and antisemitism in public schools and public postsecondary educational institutions
Reestablishes the Second Amendment Preservation Act
Adds mifepristone to the list of Schedule IV controlled substances and establishes the offense of coerced criminal abortion by means of fraud
Modifies and establishes provisions relating to the protection of children and vulnerable persons
Designates the last week in April each year as "Infertility Awareness Week"
Proposes a constitutional amendment relating to property tax exemptions
